column10: Table 1 — note ConA 13 same ΔG as Dioclea 13

column4: Table 3 — note: Dioclea binds trimannoside MORE tightly

10.1021/bi00005a026: ΔH more negative for Dioclea than ConA by up to 4.5 kcal/mol, but TΔS compensates — ΔG similar or even reversed
Snowdrop lectin: ΔH less negative than ΔG, TΔS favorable — opposite to legume lectins
Snowdrop has distinct 1-3 and 1-6 arm sites, NOT a single high-affinity monosaccharide site
Ka ≈ 3000 M⁻¹ for both trimannoside and 1-3 dimannoside — NOT additive
ConA and Dioclea: single high-affinity site + extended site for trimannoside
Key Dioclea vs ConA difference: Thr226→Gly226 near binding site, affects Arg228 flexibility
Solvent reorganization accounts for ~25% of net binding enthalpy in protein-carbohydrate interactions
ΔH for ConA/MeαMan correlates linearly with ionic strength (r=0.987)
Scatchard plots show positive cooperativity for ConA and Dioclea with trimannoside — temperature dependent
